title: Topic, Headers, Alternate, and Exchange-to-Exchange Routing · Retrieval Circuit
summary: A delayed handoff reconstructs topic, headers, alternate, and exchange-to-exchange routing from bounded publish, route, delivery, acknowledgement, and recovery evidence, challenges one misconception, and revises the multi-strategy routing and unroutable-message table. Planned artifact: Extend the evidence record for multi-strategy routing and unroutable-message table after a delayed changed case. Learners progress through prediction, explicit theory, changed-case practice, causal debugging, and…
objectives: Design bounded dot-separated topic taxonomies whose stable semantic segments support exact, star, and hash matching.
Enumerate overlapping topic bindings and prove which queues receive one copy even when multiple bindings on the same queue match.
Use headers exchanges only with typed bounded metadata and explicit all-versus-any matching, not as a schema-free routing escape hatch.
Preserve previously learned constraints and attach observable changed-case evidence.

steps.title: RabbitMQ and TypeScript Topic Segment Design · changed case · Review Topic Segment Design
steps.instruction:
review case — A review messaging team handles case 5586 in a accessible municipal task intake with two worker pools. Build deterministic TypeScript evidence to classify primary, alternate, returned, and invalid routing outcomes; prove topology rollback before deleting the old binding; then make the broker confirmation disappear after the message may have committed. Browser code models pure messaging decisions only. amqplib, AMQP sockets, RabbitMQ, TLS, partitions, restarts, Docker, databases, external effects, load, upgrades, and production behavior require explicit authorized transfer gates.
